optimizations: Simplify part design to reduce complexity and machining time ($20-$30 savings)
Use a more efficient cutting strategy, such as using a ball nose endmill instead of a flat endmill ($15-$25 savings)
risks: High machining cost due to complex geometry (high face count, high surface area)
Potential for tool wear or breakage due to 5-axis machining of steel
Setup cost may be underestimated if additional fixtures or clamps are required
